sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Νείλου μὲν αἵδε καλλιπάρθενοι ῥοαί ,
sections.translation: These are the lovely pure streams of the Nile , which waters the plain and lands of Egypt , fed by white melting snow instead of rain from heaven . Proteus was king of this land when he was alive ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: Φάρον μὲν οἰκῶν νῆσον , Αἰγύπτου δʼ ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: living on the island of Pharos and lord of Egypt ; and he married one of the daughters of the sea , Psamathe , after she left Aiakos ’ bed . She bore two children in his palace here : a son Theoklymenos , because he spent his life in reverence of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: βίον διήνεγκʼ εὐγενῆ τε παρθένον
sections.translation: and a noble daughter , her mother ’ s pride , called Eido in her infancy . But when she came to youth , the season of marriage , she was called Theonoe ; for she knew whatever the gods design , both present and to come ,

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: προγόνου λαβοῦσα Νηρέως τιμὰς πάρα .
sections.translation: having received this honor from her grandfather Nereus .

sections.label: Section 16
sections.original: ἡμῖν δὲ γῆ μὲν πατρὶς οὐκ ἀνώνυμος
sections.translation: My own fatherland , Sparta , is not without fame , and my father is Tyndareus ; but there is indeed a story that Zeus flew to my mother Leda , taking the form of a bird , a swan ,

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: ὃς δόλιον εὐνὴν ἐξέπραξʼ ὑπʼ αἰετοῦ
sections.translation: which accomplished the deceitful union , fleeing the pursuit of an eagle , if this story is true . My name is Helen ; I will tell the evils I have suffered . For the sake of beauty , three goddesses came to a deep valley on Mount Ida , to Paris :

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: Ἥρα Κύπρις τε διογενής τε παρθένος ,
sections.translation: Hera and Kypris , and the virgin daughter of Zeus , wishing to have the judgment of their loveliness decided . Kypris offered my beauty , if misfortune is beautiful , for Paris to marry , and so she won . Paris , the shepherd of Ida , left his ox-stalls

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: Σπάρτην ἀφίκεθʼ ὡς ἐμὸν σχήσων λέχος .
sections.translation: and came to Sparta , to have me in marriage .

sections.label: Section 31
sections.original: Ἥρα δὲ μεμφθεῖσʼ οὕνεκʼ οὐ νικᾷ θεάς ,
sections.translation: But Hera , indignant at not defeating the goddesses , made an airy nothing of my marriage with Paris ; she gave to the son of king Priam not me , but an image , alive and breathing , that she fashioned out of the sky and made to look like me ;

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: Πριάμου τυράννου παιδί · καὶ δοκεῖ μʼ ἔχειν —
sections.translation: and he thinks he has me — an idle fancy , for he doesn ’ t have me . And in turn the plans of Zeus added further troubles to these ; for he brought a war upon the land of the Hellenes and the unhappy Phrygians , so that he might lighten mother earth

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: πλήθους τε κουφίσειε μητέρα χθόνα
sections.translation: of her crowded mass of mortals , and bring fame to the bravest man of Hellas . So I was set up as the Hellenes ’ spear-prize , to test the courage of the Trojans ; or rather not me , but my name . Hermes caught me up in the folds of the air and

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: νεφέλῃ καλύψας — οὐ γὰρ ἠμέλησέ μου
sections.translation: hid me in a cloud — for Zeus was not neglectful of me — and he set me down here in the house of Proteus , having selected the most self-controlled of all mankind , so that I might keep my bed pure for Menelaos . And so I am here , while my wretched husband

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: στράτευμʼ ἀθροίσας τὰς ἐμὰς ἀναρπαγὰς
sections.translation: has gathered an army and gone over to the towers of Ilion to hunt down and recover me . And many lives have been lost for my sake by the streams of Skamandros ; and I who have endured all this am accursed , and have in appearance betrayed my husband

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: πόσιν συνάψαι πόλεμον Ἕλλησιν μέγαν .
sections.translation: and brought a great war to the Hellenes . Why then am I still alive ? I heard the god Hermes declare that I would yet live in the glorious country of Sparta , with my husband — for Hermes knew I never went to Ilion — so that I would not go to bed with another man .

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: ἕως μὲν οὖν φῶς ἡλίου τόδʼ ἔβλεπεν
sections.translation: Well , as long as Proteus saw this light of the sun , I was safe from marriage ; but now that he is hidden in the dark earth , the dead man ’ s son hunts after a marriage with me . But I , out of regard to my husband of long ago , am throwing myself down as a suppliant before this tomb of Proteus ,

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: ἱκέτις , ἵνʼ ἀνδρὶ τἀμὰ διασῴσῃ λέχη ,
sections.translation: for him to keep my bed safe for my husband , so that , if I bear a name infamous throughout Hellas , at least my body may not incur disgrace here .

sections.label: Section 68
sections.original: τίς τῶνδʼ ἐρυμνῶν δωμάτων ἔχει κράτος ;
sections.translation: Who holds power over this fortified house ? The dwelling is worthy of comparison with Ploutos ’,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: βασίλειά τʼ ἀμφιβλήματʼ εὔθριγκοί θʼ ἕδραι .
sections.translation: its royal enclosures and towering buildings . Ah ! Oh gods , what sight is here ? I see the hateful deadly likeness of the woman who ruined me and all the Achaeans . May the gods spurn you , so much do you look like

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: Ἑλένης , ἀποπτύσειαν . εἰ δὲ μὴ ʼν ξένῃ
sections.translation: Helen ! If I were not in a foreign land , you would have died by this well-aimed arrow as a reward for your likeness to the daughter of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: τί δʼ , ὦ ταλαίπωρʼ — ὅστις ὤν μʼ ἀπεστράφης
sections.translation: What is it , poor man — who are you , that you have turned away from me and loathe me for the misfortunes of that one ?

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἥμαρτον · ὀργῇ δʼ εἶξα μᾶλλον ἤ με χρῆν ·
sections.translation: I was wrong ; I gave way to my anger more than I should , for all Hellas hates that daughter of Zeus . Forgive me for what I said , lady .

sections.label: Section 83
sections.original: τίς δʼ εἶ ; πόθεν γῆς τῆσδʼ ἐπεστράφης πέδον ;
sections.translation: Who are you ? Where have you come from , to visit this land ?

sections.label: Section 84
sections.original: εἷς τῶν Ἀχαιῶν , ὦ γύναι , τῶν ἀθλίων .
sections.translation: I am one of those unfortunate Achaeans , lady .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: οὐ τἄρα σʼ Ἑλένην εἰ στυγεῖς θαυμαστέον .
sections.translation: Then it is no wonder that you loathe Helen . But who are you and where do you come from ? Whose son should I call you ?

sections.label: Section 87
sections.original: ὄνομα μὲν ἡμῖν Τεῦκρος , ὁ δὲ φύσας πατὴρ
sections.translation: My name is Teucer , my father is Telamon , and Salamis is the land that nurtured me .

sections.label: Section 89
sections.original: τί δῆτα Νείλου τούσδʼ ἐπιστρέφῃ γύας ;
sections.translation: Then why are you visiting these lands of the Nile ?

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: φυγὰς πατρῴας ἐξελήλαμαι χθονός .
sections.translation: I am an exile , driven out of my native land .

sections.label: Section 91
sections.original: τλήμων ἂν εἴης · τίς δέ σʼ ἐκβάλλει πάτρας ;
sections.translation: You must be unhappy ! Who banished you from your fatherland ?

sections.label: Section 92
sections.original: Τελαμὼν ὁ φύσας . τίνʼ ἂν ἔχοις μᾶλλον φίλον ;
sections.translation: My father Telamon . Could you find anyone closer to me ?

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦ ; τὸ γάρ τοι πρᾶγμα συμφορὰν ἔχει .
sections.translation: But why ? This matter is surely an unfortunate one .

sections.label: Section 94
sections.original: Αἴας μʼ ἀδελφὸς ὤλεσʼ ἐν Τροίᾳ θανών .
sections.translation: The death of my brother Aias at Troy was my ruin .

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: πῶς ; οὔ τί που σῷ φασγάνῳ βίον στερείς ;
sections.translation: How so ? You didn ’ t take his life with your sword , did you ?

sections.label: Section 96
sections.original: οἰκεῖον αὐτὸν ὤλεσʼ ἅλμʼ ἐπὶ ξίφος .
sections.translation: He threw himself on his own sword and died .

sections.label: Section 97
sections.original: μανέντʼ ; ἐπεὶ τίς σωφρονῶν τλαίη τάδʼ ἄν ;
sections.translation: Was he mad ? For what sensible man would dare such a thing ?

sections.label: Section 98
sections.original: τὸν Πηλέως τινʼ οἶσθʼ Ἀχιλλέα γόνον ;
sections.translation: Do you know a certain Achilleus , the son of Peleus ?

sections.label: Section 99
sections.original: ναί ·
sections.translation: Yes ; he came to woo Helen once , so I hear .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: θανὼν ὅδʼ ὅπλων ἔριν ἔθηκε συμμάχοις .
sections.translation: When he died , he left a contest for his armor to his allies .

sections.label: Section 101
sections.original: καὶ δὴ τί τοῦτʼ Αἴαντι γίγνεται κακόν ;
sections.translation: Well , if he did , what harm is this to Aias ?

sections.label: Section 102
sections.original: ἄλλου λαβόντος ὅπλʼ ἀπηλλάχθη βίου .
sections.translation: When someone else got the arms , he took his own life .

sections.label: Section 103
sections.original: σὺ τοῖς ἐκείνου δῆτα πήμασιν νοσεῖς ;
sections.translation: Then are you ill through his suffering ?

sections.label: Section 104
sections.original: ὁθούνεκʼ αὐτῷ γʼ οὐ ξυνωλόμην ὁμοῦ .
sections.translation: Yes , because I did not die together with him .

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: ἦλθες γάρ , ὦ ξένʼ , Ἰλίου κλεινὴν πόλιν ;
sections.translation: So you went to the famous city of Ilion , stranger ?

sections.label: Section 106
sections.original: καὶ ξύν γε πέρσας αὐτὸς ἀνταπωλόμην .
sections.translation: Yes , and by helping to sack it , I destroyed myself as well .

sections.label: Section 107
sections.original: ἤδη γὰρ ἧπται καὶ κατείργασται πυρί ;
sections.translation: Has it already been set alight and completely consumed by fire ?

sections.label: Section 108
sections.original: ὥστʼ οὐδʼ ἴχνος γε τειχέων εἶναι σαφές .
sections.translation: So that not even a trace of the walls is evident .

sections.label: Section 109
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον Ἑλένη , διὰ σʼ ἀπόλλυνται Φρύγες .
sections.translation: O miserable Helen ! Because of you , the Phrygians have been destroyed .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: καὶ πρός γʼ Ἀχαιοί · μεγάλα δʼ εἴργασται κακά .
sections.translation: And also the Achaeans ; great evils have been committed .

sections.label: Section 111
sections.original: πόσον χρόνον γὰρ διαπεπόρθηται πόλις ;
sections.translation: How long is it since the city was sacked ?

sections.label: Section 112
sections.original: ἑπτὰ σχεδόν τι καρπίμους ἐτῶν κύκλους .
sections.translation: Almost seven years have gone full circle , with their harvests .

sections.label: Section 113
sections.original: χρόνον δʼ ἐμείνατʼ ἄλλον ἐν Τροίᾳ πόσον ;
sections.translation: And how much longer were you waiting at Troy ?

sections.label: Section 114
sections.original: πολλὰς σελήνας , δέκα διελθούσας ἔτη .
sections.translation: For many months ; the moon held its course through ten years .

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: ἦ καὶ γυναῖκα Σπαρτιᾶτιν εἵλετε ;
sections.translation: And did you capture the Spartan woman ?

sections.label: Section 116
sections.original: Μενέλαος αὐτὴν ἦγʼ ἐπισπάσας κόμης .
sections.translation: Menelaos caught her by the hair to drag her away .

sections.label: Section 117
sections.original: εἶδες σὺ τὴν δύστηνον ; ἢ κλύων λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Did you yourself see the wretched creature ? Or do you speak from hearsay ?

sections.label: Section 118
sections.original: ὥσπερ γε σέ , οὐδὲν ἧσσον , ὀφθαλμοῖς ὁρῶ .
sections.translation: I saw her with my own eyes , just as I see you , no less .

sections.label: Section 119
sections.original: σκοπεῖτε μὴ δόκησιν εἴχετʼ ἐκ θεῶν .
sections.translation: Consider whether you had some fancy , sent by the gods .

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: ἄλλου λόγου μέμνησο , μὴ κείνης ἔτι .
sections.translation: Think of some other topic , not that woman still !

sections.label: Section 121
sections.original: οὕτω δοκεῖτε τὴν δόκησιν ἀσφαλῆ ;
sections.translation: Are you so sure this fancy was reliable ?

sections.label: Section 122
sections.original: αὐτὸς γὰρ ὄσσοις εἰδόμην · καὶ νοῦς ὁρᾷ .
sections.translation: I saw it with my own eyes ; and the mind has sight .

sections.label: Section 123
sections.original: ἤδη δʼ ἐν οἴκοις σὺν δάμαρτι Μενέλεως ;
sections.translation: Is Menelaos already at home with his wife ?
